name: 23-cache-control-passthrough
description: OR auto-prompt-cache passthrough (#NNN): single-turn no-tool happy path with `cacheControl: { type: 'ephemeral' }` set on the scenario. The OR side forwards the directive into `OpenRouterAgentRunOptions.cacheControl` â†’ top-level `cacheControl` field on the `callModel` request body. The OR canonical projection (`script-engine.ts`'s openresponses branch) does NOT include `cache_control`, so the request hash matches the no-cache case for the same prompt/model combo (identical to scenario #01's openresponses hash). The pinned `@openrouter/sdk` (0.12.35) declares `cacheControl` on `ChatRequest` but not on `ResponsesRequest`, so the field is silently stripped at SDK serialization today â€” the plumbing is ready and exercises the agent-layer spread sites, and the field will reach the wire once the SDK declares it on `ResponsesRequest` (newer 0.12.79+). PARITY ASYMMETRY: the Claude Agent SDK's `query()` Options has NO top-level cacheControl knob (prompt caching on Anthropic is per-content-block via `cache_control` on `TextBlockParam` â€” a different mechanism), so the scenario's `cacheControl` field is intentionally OR-only. The Anthropic-side request hashes match scenario #01's exactly (no field on either wire). The parity claim is: setting `cacheControl` does not break OR-side execution and the event stream is identical to scenario #01.
